title: Pipe and Wood Slat Bed
summary: An industrial bed frame built from galvanized pipe fittings with wood slats hung between the horizontal rails on split-ring hangers — plumbing meets woodworking.
description:
This is more plumbing than carpentry: a bed frame assembled entirely from threaded galvanized pipe and fittings, with pine slats suspended between the horizontal pipe rails using split-ring pipe hangers. The look is unmistakably industrial, and the beauty of it is that nothing is welded — it all threads together, so you can take it apart to move it.

The honest truth from the build is that pipe assembly is fiddly: no glue or thread sealant on the connections, hand-tighten only, and expect to screw and unscrew things several times to get every piece landing where it should. Pipe threads mean the two ends of a rail tighten in opposite directions, so there's a give-and-take — the standard advice is to run a horizontal pipe fully into the completed leg first, then connect the other end. Have your hardware store cut and thread the long 1" pipes to length; that's not a home job.

The wood is simple: cut the slats, predrill 1-1/2" in from each outer edge, and screw a split-ring hanger there, making sure each hanger is perpendicular to the board. Slats hang off the horizontal pipes and get centered with even spacing before you tighten the hangers with equal pressure on both screws. Build the mattress platform from the 2x4 supports and furring strips separately to carry the mattress. Because it mixes fasteners, tolerances, and a lot of trial fitting, budget patience.
timeLabel: 8–12 hrs, much of it fitting pipe

tools.note: Cuts the wood slats and platform supports.
Predrill the slats for the split-ring hanger screws.

steps.title: Understand the pipe rules first
steps.body: Use no glue or sealant on any pipe or pipe-to-wood connection. Hand-tighten only until you're confident of placement — expect to screw and unscrew repeatedly to get everything aligned. Have the store cut and thread the long 1" pipes to length; that's not a job to do at home.

steps.title: Build one footboard leg
steps.body: Assemble only one leg of the footboard, screwing the fittings together until snug. Leave the rest for now — building one complete leg first gives you a fixed reference to fit everything else against.

steps.title: Prepare the second leg's foot and long rail
steps.body: Prepare only the foot portion of the other leg — the 1"x1-1/4" coupling, a 1"x8" nipple, and a tee — and connect the 1"x75" pipe to this foot portion only. This gives you the two ends to hang slats between.

steps.title: Prep the slats with hangers
steps.body: Cut the wood slats and predrill 1-1/2" in from each outer edge for two split-ring pipe hangers. Screw the hangers on with 2" screws, making sure each hanger sits perpendicular to the board.

steps.title: Hang slats and build up the rails
steps.body: Slip one end of a slat into the completed leg side, then screw in the next 1"x8" nipple — but do not tighten the pipe hangers yet. Add a tee, then a 3/4" horizontal support (run it fully into the completed leg first, then connect the other end). Center each slat with even spacing top and bottom, then tighten its hangers with equal pressure on both screws. Repeat up the frame, adding elbows and 18" pipes where the plan calls for corners.

steps.title: Build the mattress platform
steps.body: Build the mattress platform separately: attach the furring-strip cross supports to the 2x4 supports with countersunk 1-1/2" screws, spacing the strips evenly. Set the platform in the frame, add the mattress, and you're done.

images.alt: Industrial bed frame of galvanized pipe with wood slats on pipe hangers
